candidates.name: denoiser
candidates.blurb: The network diffusion and flow models train to undo corruption — a convolutional U-Net in the first wave, now usually a transformer (DiT).
candidates.note: page-debt: already a map node and builder chip, but unclickable — write the page, set the module's slug, delete this entry

sources.label: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (Ho et al., 2020)
What are Diffusion Models? (Lilian Weng, 2021)
The Annotated Diffusion Model (Hugging Face, 2022)

candidates.name: generator + discriminator
candidates.blurb: The adversarial pair: a generator forging samples, a discriminator judging real from fake, each trained against the other.
candidates.note: page-debt: map node + builder chip with no page; GAN Lab covers the training dynamics interactively — link, don't rebuild

sources.label: Generative Adversarial Networks (Goodfellow et al., 2014)
GAN Lab (Polo Club of Data Science)
From GAN to WGAN (Lilian Weng, 2017)

candidates.name: U-Net
candidates.blurb: Encoder–decoder convolutions with skip connections at every scale — the denoiser of the first diffusion wave, still the default in segmentation.
candidates.note: currently a slugless denoiser variant on the map — a page gives that variant its missing link target

sources.label: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation (Ronneberger et al., 2015)
An Overview of Semantic Image Segmentation (Jeremy Jordan, 2018)

candidates.name: LoRA
candidates.blurb: Freeze the weights, learn low-rank deltas on top — fine-tune a huge model by training under 1% of its parameters.
candidates.note: training-efficiency trio; modifier-shaped like MoE/GQA (changes how primitives run, not which ones)

sources.label: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models (Hu et al., 2021)
QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs (Dettmers et al., 2023)
Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning using PEFT (Hugging Face, 2023)

candidates.name: quantization
candidates.blurb: Store weights in fewer bits (8, 4, even 2) — most of the quality at a fraction of the memory.
candidates.note: training-efficiency trio; Grootendorst's visual guide is near-definitive — link, don't write deep

sources.label: LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale (Dettmers et al., 2022)
A Visual Guide to Quantization (Maarten Grootendorst, 2024)

candidates.name: distillation
candidates.blurb: Train a small student to match a big teacher's output distribution, not just the labels — keeping most of the capability at a fraction of the size.
candidates.note: training-efficiency trio; Hinton 2015 plus DistilBERT as the worked example

sources.label: Distilling the Knowledge in a Neural Network (Hinton et al., 2015)
DistilBERT, a Distilled Version of BERT (Sanh et al., 2019)
Large Transformer Model Inference Optimization (Lilian Weng, 2023)

candidates.name: autoregressive
candidates.blurb: predict the next token/frame given everything so far
candidates.note: objectives batch (inventory decision 2b): writing this page requires adding a slug field to objectives in explainer.json

sources.label: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (Brown et al., 2020)
The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Recurrent Neural Networks (Andrej Karpathy, 2015)

candidates.name: masked
candidates.blurb: hide pieces of the input and reconstruct them from context
candidates.note: objectives batch (inventory decision 2b): writing this page requires adding a slug field to objectives in explainer.json
